metadata.title: ShifuHealth – Traditional Chinese Medicine | TCM Blog & AI Assistant
metadata.description: Explore the healing art of Traditional Chinese Medicine. TCM knowledge, dietary advice, herbal wisdom and your personal AI assistant Shifu Qi.
metadata.keywords: Traditional Chinese Medicine, TCM, TCM blog, Qi, Yin Yang, Five Elements, TCM diet, TCM herbs, acupuncture, TCM assistant, Chinese healing, holistic health

hero.title: The Wisdom of Traditional Chinese Medicine — for your modern life
hero.subtitle: Rediscover ancient healing. Shifu Qi guides you on your path to health and balance.

tcmIntro.title: What is Traditional Chinese Medicine?
tcmIntro.qi_text: Qi is the vital life energy that flows through your body. When Qi flows freely and in balance, you feel healthy and full of vitality. Blockages can lead to discomfort.
tcmIntro.yinyang_text: Two opposing yet complementary forces — like rest and activity, cold and warmth. Health arises when Yin and Yang are in balance.
tcmIntro.elements_text: Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water — five transformation phases that connect organs, emotions and seasons.

shifuQi.title: Your Personal TCM Expert: Shifu Qi
shifuQi.body: Describe your symptoms — Shifu Qi analyses them according to TCM principles and gives you grounded recommendations tailored to your individual pattern.
shifuQi.chatUser: I often feel tired and my hands are always cold.
shifuQi.chatBot: Based on TCM principles, this could indicate a Qi and Yang deficiency. I recommend warming foods like ginger and cinnamon...

blogPreview.title: Knowledge from TCM
blogPreview.article1Title: What is Qi? An Introduction to Life Energy
blogPreview.article1Excerpt: Qi is the foundation of TCM. Learn how this vital force affects your body and how you can strengthen it.
blogPreview.article2Title: TCM Nutrition: Eating by the Five Elements
blogPreview.article2Excerpt: Learn how to support your inner balance with the right diet — adapted to the season.
blogPreview.article3Title: The 5 Most Important TCM Herbs for Beginners
blogPreview.article3Excerpt: From goji berries to astragalus: discover TCM healing herbs and their effects on your wellbeing.

fiveElements.title: The Five Elements of TCM
fiveElements.woodText: The Wood element represents growth and renewal. It nourishes creativity and flexibility.
fiveElements.fireText: Fire represents warmth, love and the joy of life. It governs the heart and the spirit.
fiveElements.earthText: Earth stands for stability and nourishment. It is the centre that connects all elements.
fiveElements.metalText: Metal stands for clarity, structure and letting go. It supports breathing and the immune system.
fiveElements.waterText: Water is the source of life. It stands for depth, wisdom and the essence of our vitality.
